result.actor_summary: In February 2026, APT28 exploited CVE-2026-21513, an MSHTML flaw, via malicious Windows Shortcut files to conduct multi-stage payload delivery operations. This represents continued Russian state-sponsored targeting activity leveraging legitimate Windows functionality for initial access and persistence mechanisms.
result.landscape_summary: The February 2026 vulnerability landscape demonstrates a continued pattern of state-sponsored actors rapidly operationalizing publicly disclosed flaws, with APT28's exploitation of the MSHTML vulnerability (CVE-2026-21513) exemplifying how Russian threat groups are integrating living-off-the-land techniques like malicious shortcut files into multi-stage attack chains. This reflects a broader shift where nation-state actors are abandoning exclusive reliance on zero-days in favor of exploiting patched vulnerabilities at scale, particularly when public proof-of-concept code becomes available within weeks of disclosure.
result.title: February 2026 CVE Landscape: 13 Critical Vulnerabilities Mark 43% Drop from January
